metadata.method: Verbatim Q+1 revenue guides mined from 9 AMAT transcripts + actuals from 8 SEC filings (2 10-K + 6 10-Q via XBRL companyfacts). Stock reactions T-1/T/T+1 from AMAT/Stock Price Data.
metadata.actuals_source: SEC EDGAR XBRL companyfacts (CIK 0000006951) — quarterly RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax + GrossProfit + OperatingIncomeLoss + NetIncomeLoss
metadata.guidance_source: AMAT/Transcripts/*.docx — Brice Hill (CFO) Q+1 revenue + GM + EPS guidance commentary

FY24.note: Partial — Q2-Q4 only in our 8Q window

key_developments: FY26-Q1: Q2 guide $7.65B (+9% seq) — first sequential acceleration in 4 quarters; GM 49.3% target.
FY25-Q4: revenue dipped sequentially to $6.8B due to China softness + nonlinear leading-edge timing.
FY25-Q3: $7.3B record; CFE e-beam crossed $1B run-rate; DRAM HBM4 traction.
Trade controls: $400M annual headwind from December 2024/January 2025 restrictions — half hit FY25-Q2.
Advanced packaging revenue tracking toward $1.7-2.0B in FY25, doubled YoY.

China / Geopolitical Risks: China revenue is 27% of FY26-Q1 sales (down from 30%+ in FY24) — concentrated exposure to export-control changes.
Trade controls announced Dec 2024 + Jan 2025 created a $400M annualized revenue headwind; first half impact landed in FY25-Q2 with continued AGS impact in H2 FY25.
Further export restrictions or Taiwan tensions could disrupt customer relationships and compress multiple.
Source: AMAT FY2025 10-K Item 1A Risk Factors + Brice Hill (CFO) FY25-Q1 commentary on the $400M headwind.
Cyclical Industry Risks: Semicap industry is cyclical — wafer-fab equipment (WFE) capex volatile across cycles; AMAT revenue swings 20-40% peak-to-trough historically.
FY25-Q4 sequential dip ($7.3B → $6.8B) demonstrated nonlinear customer timing exposure.
Multiple typically caps at 35-40x P/E even in upcycles; downside in cycle troughs has historically been -30% to -50%.
Source: AMAT 10-K Item 1A — cyclical industry risk language; cycle pattern analysis from 2000-2026 stock data.
Customer Concentration Risks: Top-5 customers (TSMC, Samsung, Intel, SK Hynix, Micron) drive >60% of revenue — order timing nonlinear and tied to capex cycles.
Customer roadmap delays (e.g., Intel 18A pushed, Samsung 2nm timing) directly impact AMAT's near-term revenue cadence.
Hyperscaler-driven HBM demand from one customer (NVIDIA via SK Hynix/Micron/Samsung) creates concentration in advanced-packaging revenue.
Source: AMAT FY2025 10-K Item 1A + Q3-Q4 FY25 transcripts noting nonlinear leading-edge customer timing.
Competitive Risks: LRCX (etch) and KLAC (inspection/metrology) are direct competitors with overlapping product portfolios.
ASML holds EUV lithography monopoly — AMAT does not compete on lithography but is exposed to ASML pacing of leading-edge nodes.
Tokyo Electron (TEL) is a global competitor across CVD/etch/coating-developer with strong relationships at Japanese + Korean fabs.
Source: FactSet peer comp + AMAT 10-K Item 1A competitive risk disclosures.
Operational Risks: Currency risk — operations in Japan, Korea, Europe; FX swings of ±5% impact margins by 50-150bps.
FY26-Q1 included an accrual charge — accounting adjustments can produce variability in reported earnings vs operational performance.
Cybersecurity + IP protection risk — semicap process know-how is highly valuable; theft or breach could erode competitive moat.
Source: AMAT 10-K Item 1A Risk Factors — Operations + Cybersecurity sections.

narrative.thesis: Applied Materials is a premier semiconductor-capital-equipment vendor benefiting from advanced-logic, AI/HBM, and DRAM compute-memory inflections. FY25 was the 6th consecutive year of revenue growth (high-single-digit), and FY26 guidance points to mid-9% sequential growth in Q2 ($7.65B guide) driven by leading-edge foundry/logic, advanced packaging (HBM, hybrid bonding) and CFE e-beam.
bull_case: FY26-Q2 revenue guide $7.65B (+9% sequential, +7% YoY) — best sequential acceleration in 4 quarters.
Gross margin guide 49.3% — highest since FY2000, driven by mix shift to advanced foundry/logic + DRAM.
CFE e-beam revenue expected to double in CY26 to >$1B; advanced packaging growing toward $2B run-rate.
Wins at leading-edge gate-all-around (>50% process equipment share); DRAM revenue ramping w/ HBM and DDR5.
Net cash on balance sheet (~$1.3B net cash) + ongoing buyback program.
bear_case: China revenue down 7% YoY; export restrictions continue to be a $400M-class annual headwind.
FY25-Q4 was sequentially down (-7% revenue); revenue durability through cycle troughs unproven.
Multiple is mid-30s P/E FY1 (below peer avg of 39x) — but absolute multiple still rich for a cyclical.
ICAPS (mature-node) demand is flat; structural shift away from non-leading-edge could pressure mix.
Stock has rallied from $153 (52w low) to $437 (~+185%); near-term technical extension risk.
triggers: FY26-Q2 print (5/15): confirm $7.65B guide hit + GM 49.3%; advanced packaging revenue trajectory.
AMAT analyst day (typically Sept): updated long-term framework + China outlook.
Earnings cadence: confirms FY26 mid-single-digit growth thesis or breaks it on China headwinds.
narrative.bottom_line: AMAT shows clean execution on revenue guidance (8/8 in-line, 98.6% info-adj MCS) and is benefiting from AI/HBM tailwinds. FY26-Q2 guide signals re-acceleration. Trading at FY1 P/E ~36x (modest discount to semicap peers); valuation supportive if FY26 mid-single-digit growth holds. Bull case stronger near-term given Q2 acceleration narrative; bear case rests on China exposure normalization.

fundamentals: Net margin 28.9% (best-in-class for Information Technology; sector strong-bar 18%)
valuation: PE 36.2× vs peer median 37.5× (4% discount to peers)

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Next-quarter consensus: $7.41B (+5.6% vs current Q) — set by 25 broker contributors.
Margin context: 28.9% net margin (26.1% op margin) — expansion likely.
Next earnings: 2026-05-15 (estimate, FY26-Q2) — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $28B (+2.1% TTM YoY) — mature / saturating.
Sell-side: FY1 $33B → FY2 $41B (+25.1% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
8-quarter run-rate CAGR: +3.1% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 162.0% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 1.06 — balanced flow.
30-day implied move: ±16.9% ($362.72 – $510.52) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.

short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $415 (5% OTM). High IV (162%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 58.7% across 11 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 11 dates spanning 99–617 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±32.3% ($295.42 – $577.82).
399-day implied move: ±59.8% ($175.43 – $697.81) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $514 (+17.8% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-103.3pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Buy 1-year LEAPS call at $393 (10% ITM, 70-delta proxy). Long-cycle trend up + sell-side sees +17.8% upside to $514 PT → ITM LEAPS gives ~80% of stock exposure at ~40% of capital, with limited downside vs holding.
